title: GitHub - Pycomet/agent-atlas: Map your AI coding setup — see what your agents and skills are actually good at, what you never use, and what's missing. npx agent-atlas-cli
excerpt: Map your AI coding setup — see what your agents and skills are actually good at, what you never use, and what's missing. npx agent-atlas-cli - Pycomet/agent-atlas
content_html:
A map of your AI setup. Agent Atlas scans your AI coding environment and turns it into an interactive mind map — so you can see, at a glance, what your agents and skills are actually good at, what you never use, and what's missing.

You've probably installed dozens of skills, subagents, and MCP servers into your AI tools. But do you actually know what your setup can do? Agent Atlas reads your configuration and your session history, then draws your whole stack as a living map: every skill, agent, and tool as a node, grouped by what it's for, sized by how often you actually use it.

In one picture you can answer questions you currently can't:

  • What is my setup tuned for? More engineering than writing? Any research capability at all?
  • What am I paying for but never using? Every installed MCP server loads its tool schemas into context in every session — unused servers silently cost you tokens every single day.
  • Where are the overlaps and gaps? Two skills doing nearly the same job; whole capability areas with zero coverage.

Quick start

npx agent-atlas-cli

That's it. No config, no account. Works on Claude Code setups today (Cursor and friends are on the roadmap — the scanner is built behind an adapter interface).

Don't have an Anthropic API key handy? It still works:

npx agent-atlas-cli --rough   # keyword-based classification, no API call at all

Privacy — read this part

Agent Atlas is read-only and local-only:

  • It never modifies anything on your machine.
  • Nothing leaves your machine except one optional classification API call — and that call sends only the names and descriptions of your skills/agents/servers. Your session transcripts, your code, and your prompts are never sent anywhere.
  • With --rough, nothing leaves your machine at all.
  • It's open source (MIT). Don't take our word for any of this — read the code.

How it works

Four stages, one pipeline:

Scanner  →  Usage Miner  →  Classifier  →  Renderer
(what's      (what actually   (what each     (the map +
installed)    fires)           piece is for)   diagnostics)
  1. Scanner — inventories skills, subagents, MCP servers, and hooks from your ~/.claude configuration (plus the current project's .claude/).
  2. Usage Miner — streams your local session transcripts and counts what actually fired in the last 30 days (--days to change the window).
  3. Classifier — one cheap LLM pass scores every item across five capability axes: engineering, writing, research, design, ops. Results are cached by content hash, so re-runs are fast and nearly free. Got a classification wrong? Pin the right one in ~/.agent-atlas/overrides.json — overrides always win.
  4. Renderer — draws the interactive map: clusters by capability, node size = usage, grey = dead weight, plus a "tuning bar" summarizing your whole stack (Engineering 61% · Research 17% · …). Below the map, three diagnostic lists: dead weight (with estimated tokens wasted per session), overlaps (near-duplicate skills/agents), and gaps (capability axes you barely cover). The "Share card" button exports a PNG:

Usage

npx agent-atlas-cli                  # scan, classify, open the map
npx agent-atlas-cli --json           # dump inventory + usage + classification as JSON
npx agent-atlas-cli --days 90        # widen the usage window
npx agent-atlas-cli --rough          # skip the API, use keyword heuristics
npx agent-atlas-cli --atlas-dir DIR  # custom location for cache + overrides

Classification uses your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable if set; otherwise it falls back to rough mode automatically.

Status / roadmap

Milestone What Status
M1 Scanner + Usage Miner (--json output) ✅ done
M2 Classifier — LLM pass, cache, overrides, no-key fallback ✅ done
M3 Renderer — interactive map + tuning bar (atlas.html) ✅ done
M4 Diagnostics (dead weight, overlaps, gaps) + shareable card ✅ done
v2 Adapters for Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI; recommendations 💭 planned

The full design lives in SPEC.md.

Development

npm install
npm run build     # tsc → dist/
npm test          # vitest, runs against the fixture tree in fixtures/

All tests run against a fake ~/.claude tree in fixtures/ — nothing in the test suite touches your real setup. If you're adding a scanner or classifier change, extend the fixtures and the expected outputs alongside it.

Releasing

Merging to main runs CI only. Publishing to npm happens when a version tag is pushed:

npm version patch    # or minor / major — bumps package.json, commits, tags vX.Y.Z
git push origin main --follow-tags

The publish workflow then verifies the tag matches package.json, runs the test suite, builds, and publishes to npm via trusted publishing (OIDC) — no tokens involved.

Contributions welcome — especially adapter implementations for other AI coding tools and hand-labeled classification examples for the rubric.

Teams

Curious what your whole engineering team's AI stack looks like — aggregate maps, redundant spend, shadow tooling? I'm exploring a team version. Email alfredemmanuelinyang@gmail.com.

License

MIT © 2026 Alfred Emmanuel

sentences.en: Four stages, one pipeline:

blocks.html_en: • Scanner — inventories skills, subagents, MCP servers, and hooks from your ~/.claude configuration (plus the current project's .claude/).

sentences.en: Scanner — inventories skills, subagents, MCP servers, and hooks from your ~/.claude configuration (plus the current project's .claude/).
sentences.zh: 扫描器 — 库存技能、子代理、MCP 服务器以及 ~/.claude 配置中的挂钩（加上当前项目的 .claude/）。

blocks.html_en: • Usage Miner — streams your local session transcripts and counts what actually fired in the last 30 days (--days to change the window).

sentences.en: Usage Miner — streams your local session transcripts and counts what actually fired in the last 30 days (--days to change the window).
sentences.zh: 使用矿工 — 流式传输您的本地会话记录并计算过去 30 天内实际触发的内容（--更改窗口的天数）。

blocks.html_en: • Classifier — one cheap LLM pass scores every item across five capability axes: engineering, writing, research, design, ops. Results are cached by content hash, so re-runs are fast and nearly free. Got a classification wrong? Pin the right one in ~/.agent-atlas/overrides.json — overrides always win.

sentences.en: Classifier — one cheap LLM pass scores every item across five capability axes: engineering, writing, research, design, ops.
sentences.zh: 分类器——一次廉价的法学硕士通过即可对五个能力轴的每一项进行评分：工程、写作、研究、设计、运营。

sentences.en: Results are cached by content hash, so re-runs are fast and nearly free.
sentences.zh: 结果通过内容哈希进行缓存，因此重新运行速度很快且几乎免费。

sentences.en: Got a classification wrong?
sentences.zh: 是不是分类错了？

sentences.en: Pin the right one in ~/.agent-atlas/overrides.json — overrides always win.
sentences.zh: 将正确的项固定在 ~/.agent-atlas/overrides.json 中 — 覆盖总是获胜。

blocks.html_en: • Renderer — draws the interactive map: clusters by capability, node size = usage, grey = dead weight, plus a "tuning bar" summarizing your whole stack (Engineering 61% · Research 17% · …). Below the map, three diagnostic lists: dead weight (with estimated tokens wasted per session), overlaps (near-duplicate skills/agents), and gaps (capability axes you barely cover). The "Share card" button exports a PNG:

sentences.en: Renderer — draws the interactive map: clusters by capability, node size = usage, grey = dead weight, plus a "tuning bar" summarizing your whole stack (Engineering 61% · Research 17% · …).
sentences.zh: 渲染器 — 绘制交互式地图：按功能进行聚类，节点大小 = 使用情况，灰色 = 自重，加上总结整个堆栈的“调整栏”（工程 61% · 研究 17% · …）。

sentences.en: Below the map, three diagnostic lists: dead weight (with estimated tokens wasted per session), overlaps (near-duplicate skills/agents), and gaps (capability axes you barely cover).
sentences.zh: 地图下方是三个诊断列表：自重（每个会话浪费的估计令牌）、重叠（几乎重复的技能/代理）和差距（您几乎覆盖的能力轴）。

sentences.en: The "Share card" button exports a PNG:
sentences.zh: “共享卡”按钮导出 PNG：

sentences.en: Classification uses your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable if set; otherwise it falls back to rough mode automatically.
sentences.zh: 分类使用您的 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY 环境变量（如果已设置）；否则它会自动退回到粗糙模式。

blocks.html_en: All tests run against a fake ~/.claude tree in fixtures/ — nothing in the test suite touches your real setup. If you're adding a scanner or classifier change, extend the fixtures and the expected outputs alongside it.

sentences.en: All tests run against a fake ~/.claude tree in fixtures/ — nothing in the test suite touches your real setup.
sentences.zh: 所有测试都针对fixtures/中的假~/.claude树运行——测试套件中没有任何内容触及您的真实设置。

sentences.zh: 如果您要添加扫描仪或分类器更改，请扩展灯具及其旁边的预期输出。

sentences.en: Merging to main runs CI only.

blocks.html_en: The publish workflow then verifies the tag matches package.json, runs the test suite, builds, and publishes to npm via trusted publishing (OIDC) — no tokens involved.

sentences.en: The publish workflow then verifies the tag matches package.json, runs the test suite, builds, and publishes to npm via trusted publishing (OIDC) — no tokens involved.
sentences.zh: 然后，发布工作流程会验证标签与 package.json 是否匹配、运行测试套件、构建并通过可信发布 (OIDC) 发布到 npm — 不涉及令牌。

content_html_zh:
AI 设置的地图。 Agent Atlas 会扫描您的 AI 编码环境，并将其转变为交互式思维导图，这样您就可以一目了然地了解您的代理和技能实际上擅长什么、您从未使用过什么以及缺少什么。

您可能已经在 AI 工具中安装了数十个技能、子代理和 MCP 服务器。 但您真的知道您的设置可以做什么吗？ Agent Atlas 读取您的配置和会话历史记录，然后将您的整个堆栈绘制为动态地图：每个技能、代理和工具作为一个节点，按其用途进行分组，根据您实际使用它的频率调整大小。

在一张图片中，您可以回答目前无法回答的问题：

  • 我的设置是为了什么而调整的？ 工程比写作更重要？ 有研究能力吗？
  • 我付了钱却从未使用过什么？ 每个已安装的 MCP 服务器都会将其工具模式加载到每个会话的上下文中 - 未使用的服务器每天都会默默地消耗您的令牌。
  • 重叠和差距在哪里？ 两种技能几乎完成相同的工作；全能力领域零覆盖。

快速启动

npx agent-atlas-cli

就是这样。 没有配置，没有帐户。 目前适用于 Claude Code 设置（Cursor 和朋友已在路线图上 - 扫描仪构建在适配器接口后面）。

手头没有 Anthropic API 密钥？ 它仍然有效：

npx agent-atlas-cli --rough   # keyword-based classification, no API call at all

隐私 — 阅读这部分

Agent Atlas 是只读且仅限本地的：

  • 它永远不会修改您机器上的任何内容。
  • 除了一个可选的分类 API 调用之外，您的机器不会留下任何内容，并且该调用仅发送您的技能/代理/服务器的名称和描述。 您的会话记录、代码和提示永远不会发送到任何地方。
  • 使用--rough，什么都不会离开你的机器。
  • 它是开源的（麻省理工学院）。 不要相信我们的话——阅读代码。

它是如何运作的

四阶段，一条管道：

Scanner  →  Usage Miner  →  Classifier  →  Renderer
(what's      (what actually   (what each     (the map +
installed)    fires)           piece is for)   diagnostics)
  1. 扫描器 — 库存技能、子代理、MCP 服务器以及 ~/.claude 配置中的挂钩（加上当前项目的 .claude/）。
  2. 使用矿工 — 流式传输您的本地会话记录并计算过去 30 天内实际触发的内容（--更改窗口的天数）。
  3. 分类器——一次廉价的法学硕士通过即可对五个能力轴的每一项进行评分：工程、写作、研究、设计、运营。 结果通过内容哈希进行缓存，因此重新运行速度很快且几乎免费。 是不是分类错了？ 将正确的项固定在 ~/.agent-atlas/overrides.json 中 — 覆盖总是获胜。
  4. 渲染器 — 绘制交互式地图：按功能进行聚类，节点大小 = 使用情况，灰色 = 自重，加上总结整个堆栈的“调整栏”（工程 61% · 研究 17% · …）。 地图下方是三个诊断列表：自重（每个会话浪费的估计令牌）、重叠（几乎重复的技能/代理）和差距（您几乎覆盖的能力轴）。 “共享卡”按钮导出 PNG：

Usage

npx agent-atlas-cli                  # scan, classify, open the map
npx agent-atlas-cli --json           # dump inventory + usage + classification as JSON
npx agent-atlas-cli --days 90        # widen the usage window
npx agent-atlas-cli --rough          # skip the API, use keyword heuristics
npx agent-atlas-cli --atlas-dir DIR  # custom location for cache + overrides

分类使用您的 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY 环境变量（如果已设置）；否则它会自动退回到粗糙模式。

状态/路线图

里程碑 What 地位
M1 扫描仪 + 使用矿工（--json 输出） ✅ done
M2 分类器 — LLM pass、缓存、覆盖、无键回退 ✅ done
M3 渲染器 — 交互式地图 + 调整栏 (atlas.html) ✅ done
M4 诊断（自重、重叠、间隙）+可共享卡 ✅ done
v2 Cursor、Codex CLI、Gemini CLI 适配器；建议 💭 计划中

完整的设计位于 SPEC.md 中。

发展

npm install
npm run build     # tsc → dist/
npm test          # vitest, runs against the fixture tree in fixtures/

所有测试都针对fixtures/中的假~/.claude树运行——测试套件中没有任何内容触及您的真实设置。 如果您要添加扫描仪或分类器更改，请扩展灯具及其旁边的预期输出。

释放

仅合并到主要运行 CI。 当推送版本标签时，就会发布到 npm：

npm version patch    # or minor / major — bumps package.json, commits, tags vX.Y.Z
git push origin main --follow-tags

然后，发布工作流程会验证标签与 package.json 是否匹配、运行测试套件、构建并通过可信发布 (OIDC) 发布到 npm — 不涉及令牌。

欢迎贡献——特别是其他人工智能编码工具的适配器实现和该标题的手工标记分类示例。
